name: research_ethics_satellite_derived_southport_tru_loy
premise: Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: research ethics satellite derived southport tru loy. The moral tension is explicitly framed as truth versus loyalty: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. Two upstream teams have published contradictory status pages; customers are comparing timestamps. The breach-notification and customer-communication clock has started; executive staff expect a single coherent position before the next press cycle. Preserve chain-of-custody for evidence; no bulk deletion or retention-policy changes during the incident. Any exception must be time-boxed, named, and reversible where technically feasible.

agents.name: Jonah Ishikawa
agents.identity: I am Jonah Ishikawa, the accountable decision-maker on this Research Ethics bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes loyalty whenever someone invokes truth.

agents.name: Keisha Vasquez
agents.identity: I am Keisha Vasquez, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so loyalty is not sacrificed by default just because truth feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect loyalty.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to loyalty to secure truth before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The clock on this bridge is running out. With partial evidence, Jonah Ishikawa must decide under truth versus loyalty. What does Jonah Ishikawa do?
